title: Project Telos Second-Wave Source-Checkout Tooling Claims

claims.text: The current MCP-exposed flagship status surfaces report all five Project Telos flagships as ready with a 70-tool Telos room surface.
claims.falsification: A current flagship MCP status or Telos room check reports a non-MATCH status, fewer than five ready flagships, or a tool count other than 70.

claims.text: The Learn source checkout supports a receipt-backed research-training loop: status reports version 1.5.0, doctor reports MATCH, the targeted tutor/prooflesson/reverify test slice passes, and a temp study receipt verifies from evidence.
claims.falsification: Learn status or doctor fails, the targeted test slice fails, or the temp study receipt does not report verified true.

claims.text: The BuildLang/buildc source checkout supports a receipt-backed compiler demo from an isolated Cargo target: doctor completes for buildc 1.0.6, the C backend is ready, hello.bld check emits a passed receipt, and corpus verify reports 8 C executions passed.
claims.falsification: The isolated-target doctor fails, the C backend is not ready, hello.bld does not emit a passed receipt, or corpus verify does not report 8 C executions passed.

claims.text: The Emet source checkout supports a narrow external-witness demo: selftest emits the current self hash and conformance passes 35 of 35 vectors, while the PATH wrapper remains out of scope because it still depends on a missing warden_shell module.
claims.falsification: Emet selftest fails, the conformance suite reports fewer than 35 passing vectors, or the handoff claims the broken PATH wrapper is fixed.

claims.text: The second-wave outreach documents preserve the source-checkout boundary and add demo packages for Learn, BuildLang/buildc, Emet, and megatool composition.
claims.falsification: The second-wave docs are missing, omit the PATH/source-checkout boundary, or promote unverified PATH installs.
